format_phases.notes: pre-split tight face on Nick, no B-roll yet
top ~50% SKILLS logo B-roll + bottom ~50% face close-up
full-frame B-roll screenshots, face not visible; captions persist
return to tight face, caption style switches to white/no-pill for the CTA

events.label: Tight face close-up of Nick, white wall + black acoustic panels behind, shotgun mic bottom

events.text: If you're using Claude code but not using skills, you're basically missing out on 90% of the fun.

events.style: white sans-serif on dark translucent rounded-rect pill, 2-3 words per beat

events.description: Hard cut from face close-up into split-screen with SKILLS logo appearing top half

events.label: Split screen: SKILLS logo B-roll over Nick's face
'SKILLS — THE OPEN AGENT SKILLS ECOSYSTEM' logo lockup on black background, letters in white distressed/stencil font with subtle RGB-glitch fringe
Red diagonal slash drawing across SKILLS logo (upper-right → lower-left), indicating 'NOT using skills'

events.text: Skills are like superpowers for AI, so instead of writing the same long prompts over and over again,

events.text: A massive new library just dropped that is over 1,000 pre-made Claude skills that you can install

events.text: and use instantly for completely free. These skills turn Claude into a specialist for whatever

events.text: task you have, like UI design, app testing, SEO, content writing, and much more.

events.description: Hard cut, swap card to ai-seo skill

events.text: And the best part, you can also run them inside of anti-gravity, codex, the Claude desktop app,

Green-accent bullet list of primary agents (Antigravity, Cline, Codex, Cursor, Deep Agents, Firebender, Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot, Kimi Code CLI, OpenCode, Warp), then 'Additional agents' separator, Search field with cursor, radio-button list (Augment, IBM Bob, Claude Code, OpenClaw, CodeBuddy, Command Code, Continue, Cortex Code + 'Selected: Amp, Antigravity, Cline +9 more'). Selected items highlighted in white box

events.text: and basically any AI code editor out there. Just select the skill you want, copy its command,

Hard cut to empty Administrator Command Prompt window

events.label: Windows 10 Administrator Command Prompt header + 'Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.26200.0117]' banner + prompt 'C:\Users\shahb>' with blinking cursor, hand-cursor mouse icon mid-frame

events.text: paste it into your terminal, choose your AI agent, and that's it, your new skill is ready to use.

events.description: Full-white 1–2 frame flash between CMD window and final face close-up

events.label: Tight close-up of Nick for CTA beat — eyes closed/blinking briefly at 41s, then direct-to-camera

events.text: So go try it yourself, just comment Claude down below and I'll send you the link directly.

events.style: bold white sans-serif with thin black outline, no background pill, center-lower placement, 2-3 words per beat
events.note: Caption style shifts from dark-pill Submagic (used in beats 0–41s) to white-outline-only for the CTA to feel more direct-camera and less 'overlay-y'

style_summary.concept: skills-library announcement with real-UI demo cuts + single face bookend
signature_elements: high-density cuts (12 scene changes in ~45 s ≈ one every 3.7 s)
clean minimalist indoor backdrop (white wall + black acoustic foam panels) — opposite of Devin's warm studio
shotgun mic visible bottom-center of face close-ups
split-screen B-roll-on-top + face-on-bottom ONLY as the opening setup (1.4–4.2 s); switches to full-bleed B-roll for the body of the video
Submagic-style 2–3 word captions with dark translucent rounded-rect pill for the entire body
CTA caption style switches to white-outline-only (no pill) in the final ~4 s for direct-camera intimacy
red diagonal slash drawn across the SKILLS logo during 'NOT using' moment — simple but effective negation graphic
real platform screenshots (SKILLS home, skill cards, Claude desktop prompt, Windows terminal, macOS desktop ZIP) — no mocks
animated selection pulses / cursor-hand interactions inside screenshots to guide eye
hard cuts only — no glitch, flash, or gradient transitions (except one subtle white flash at 40.96 s between CMD and final face)
style_summary.caption_philosophy: Submagic-dense. Every beat captioned with 2–3 words. Dark pill for most, white-outline for CTA only.
style_summary.color_accent: #E1251C red (only used once, on the negation slash). Otherwise neutral monochrome.
style_summary.contrast_with_devin1: Higher cut density, no emphasis-caption slams, Submagic word-by-word captions (Devin has sparse narrative-pivot-only), minimalist vs. warm studio, split-screen used only for opening setup vs. not at all in Devin
